articles.headline: Government Unveils 'India Innovation & Manufacturing Boost' (IIMB) Policy to Catalyse Domestic Production and R&D
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the ambitious 'India Innovation & Manufacturing Boost' (IIMB) policy, aimed at significantly enhancing domestic manufacturing capabilities and fostering a robust ecosystem for research and development.
• The policy introduces a package of incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) for new sectors, tax breaks for R&D investments, and easier access to credit for MSMEs in manufacturing.
• A key focus is on integrating advanced technologies like AI, IoT, and automation into traditional manufacturing processes to improve efficiency and competitiveness.
• The IIMB policy seeks to create millions of new jobs, reduce import dependency, and position India as a global manufacturing hub, aligning with the 'Make in India' vision.
• Special provisions are included to promote green manufacturing practices and circular economy principles within the industrial sector.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Industrial Policy
Make in India
Investment models
prelimsPoints: IIMB policy is launched by the Union Government.
Aims to boost domestic manufacturing and R&D.
Includes PLI schemes and tax breaks for R&D.
Focuses on integrating AI, IoT, automation.
Promotes green manufacturing and circular economy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of the IIMB policy to transform India's manufacturing sector and its contribution to GDP.
Analyze the challenges in implementing such a large-scale policy and suggest measures for effective execution.
Evaluate the role of technology integration (AI, IoT) in enhancing India's industrial competitiveness under the new policy.
Examine how the IIMB policy aligns with broader government initiatives like 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of factories with gears and innovation symbols, representing industrial growth and technological advancement.
articles.trivia: India aims to increase the manufacturing sector's share in GDP to 25% by 2025 under the National Manufacturing Policy, a goal further supported by initiatives like IIMB.

articles.headline: Parliament Winter Session: Heated Debates on Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Progress
articles.explainer:
• The ongoing Winter Session of Parliament witnessed intense discussions and debates today regarding the potential enactment and implications of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
• Members from various political parties presented their arguments, with proponents emphasizing national unity and gender justice, while opponents raised concerns about religious freedom and cultural diversity.
• The Law Commission's recent consultations and recommendations on UCC were frequently cited during the parliamentary proceedings.
• The government reiterated its commitment to Article 44 of the Constitution, which mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC for its citizens.
• The debate highlighted the complex socio-political landscape surrounding personal laws in India and the challenges in achieving consensus.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: UCC is mentioned in Article 44 of the Indian Constitution.
It is a Directive Principle of State Policy (DPSP).
Aims for common personal laws for all citizens, regardless of religion.
Law Commission of India advises the government on legal reforms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional validity and socio-political implications of implementing a Uniform Civil Code in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against UCC, considering aspects of gender justice, national integration, and protection of minority rights.
Examine the role of the judiciary and the legislature in progressing towards a UCC, citing past judgments and debates.
Suggest a roadmap for achieving a consensual and inclusive Uniform Civil Code in a diverse country like India.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Parliament House of India, symbolizing legislative debates and policy-making.
articles.trivia: Goa is currently the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from its Portuguese colonial past.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizing Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded today in New Delhi, reaffirming the commitment of both nations to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and counter-terrorism efforts, alongside exploring new avenues for collaboration in critical technologies and supply chain resilience.
• Both sides stressed the importance of upholding international law, particularly the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), in addressing regional challenges.
• Economic partnerships, including infrastructure development projects in India and trade facilitation, were also key agenda items.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan in navigating the complex geopolitical landscape of Asia.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India and Japan hold annual Strategic Dialogues.
Focus on Indo-Pacific security and economic ties.
Both nations are members of the QUAD grouping.
Cooperation areas include defense, maritime security, technology, and infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan strategic partnership for regional stability and economic development in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how India's 'Act East Policy' and Japan's 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' vision converge to address geopolitical challenges.
Evaluate the progress and potential of defense and security cooperation between India and Japan, particularly in the maritime domain.
Examine the role of India-Japan collaboration in building resilient supply chains and promoting critical technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed together, symbolizing diplomatic relations and cooperation.
articles.trivia: Japan is one of the largest investors in India, particularly in infrastructure projects like the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail corridor.

articles.headline: New Tiger Census Data Released: Highlights Challenges in Conservation Efforts Despite Population Growth
articles.explainer:
• The latest All India Tiger Estimation (AITE) report, commonly known as the Tiger Census, was released today, indicating a continued increase in India's tiger population.
• While the overall numbers show a positive trend, the report highlighted significant regional disparities and emerging challenges such as habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching.
• The census employed advanced methodologies, including camera trapping and DNA analysis, to provide a more accurate count and ecological insights.
• Conservationists called for enhanced protection measures, community involvement, and corridor connectivity to ensure the long-term survival of tigers outside protected areas.
• The report serves as a crucial benchmark for evaluating the effectiveness of 'Project Tiger' and informs future conservation strategies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Wildlife Protection Act
prelimsPoints: Tiger Census is conducted every four years in India.
NTCA is responsible for Project Tiger and tiger conservation.
India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
Methods include camera trapping, DNA analysis, and pugmark estimation.
Challenges include habitat loss, poaching, and human-wildlife conflict.
mainsPoints: Analyze the successes and failures of Project Tiger based on the latest census data, highlighting key regions of concern.
Discuss the socio-economic dimensions of human-wildlife conflict and suggest sustainable solutions for co-existence.
Evaluate the role of protected areas and wildlife corridors in maintaining genetic diversity and ensuring tiger population viability.
Examine how climate change and developmental projects pose threats to tiger habitats and conservation efforts in India.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger in its natural habitat, symbolizing wildlife conservation.
articles.trivia: India's tiger population has more than doubled since the launch of Project Tiger in 1973, making it one of the most successful conservation programs globally.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
• The test involved a precise landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, demonstrating key technologies for autonomous navigation, guidance, and control.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aimed at drastically reducing the cost of space access.
• The RLV-TD program is part of ISRO's long-term vision to enhance India's capabilities in space transportation and make space missions more economical and sustainable.
• Future tests will focus on orbital re-entry and hypersonic flight capabilities, paving the way for advanced space exploration and satellite deployment.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested its RLV-TD.
RLV aims to reduce space launch costs.
Test involved autonomous landing simulation.
RLV-TD is a step towards a fully reusable orbital vehicle.
ISRO is India's national space agency.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze how RLV technology can contribute to making space missions more economical and sustainable.
Examine the potential applications of ISRO's RLV program, including satellite deployment, human spaceflight, and deep space exploration.
Compare India's RLV efforts with similar initiatives by other leading space agencies and private companies worldwide.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) descending for a landing.
articles.trivia: The RLV-TD program began in 2016 with its first successful hypersonic flight experiment, demonstrating the vehicle's ability to survive re-entry into the atmosphere.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns, Signals Cautious Approach
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today announced its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged, maintaining a cautious stance amidst persistent inflationary pressures.
• This decision aligns with market expectations, as the central bank prioritizes price stability while also supporting economic growth.
• The MPC noted the global economic uncertainties and geopolitical tensions continue to pose risks to the inflation outlook and overall financial stability.
• The RBI indicated that it would continue to monitor liquidity conditions and remain agile in its monetary policy stance to address evolving economic dynamics.
• Experts suggest that future rate actions will be highly dependent on the trajectory of retail inflation and global commodity prices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
Inflation and its impact
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC decides on key interest rates.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks.
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability.
Inflation targeting framework guides MPC decisions.
RBI uses various tools for liquidity management.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its potential impact on inflation and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing price stability with supporting economic recovery.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and controlling inflation in the current economic scenario.
Examine the interplay between global economic trends, commodity prices, and India's domestic monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) headquarters building, symbolizing financial policy.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Issues Comprehensive Guidelines on Prisoner Rights and Access to Legal Aid
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a landmark judgment, issuing a comprehensive set of guidelines aimed at safeguarding the rights of prisoners and ensuring their effective access to legal aid.
• The directives include mandates for timely legal representation from the moment of arrest, regular medical check-ups, and provisions for humane treatment within correctional facilities.
• The Court emphasized the importance of prison reforms, urging both central and state governments to allocate adequate resources for improving prison infrastructure and staff training.
• The judgment also highlighted the need for greater transparency in prison administration and the establishment of independent oversight mechanisms.
• This ruling is expected to have a far-reaching impact on India's criminal justice system, particularly in addressing issues of overcrowding and human rights violations in prisons.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
keyTerms: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court issued guidelines on prisoner rights.
Mandates timely legal representation and humane treatment.
Stresses on prison reforms and resource allocation.
Emphasizes transparency and independent oversight in prisons.
Article 21 guarantees right to life and personal liberty.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional basis and significance of the Supreme Court's guidelines on prisoner rights and legal aid.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Indian prison system, such as overcrowding, understaffing, and human rights violations, and how these guidelines address them.
Evaluate the role of legal aid services in ensuring access to justice for vulnerable sections, particularly undertrial prisoners.
Suggest comprehensive reforms for the Indian criminal justice system, encompassing policing, judiciary, and correctional administration, in light of the Supreme Court's directives.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building, representing judicial pronouncements and justice.
articles.trivia: Article 39A of the Indian Constitution directs the State to provide free legal aid to ensure that opportunities for securing justice are not denied to any citizen by reason of economic or other disabilities.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Supreme Court judgments and their impact

articles.headline: Coastal Security Enhanced with New Surveillance Systems Along Western Coastline
articles.explainer:
• India's coastal security apparatus received a significant boost today with the inauguration of advanced surveillance systems along critical stretches of the Western coastline, particularly near the Western Ghats region.
• The new systems integrate radar technology, electro-optic sensors, and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to create a comprehensive real-time monitoring network.
• This initiative aims to bolster defense against illegal infiltration, smuggling, and maritime terrorism, enhancing the overall security of India's vast coastline.
• The Indian Navy, Coast Guard, and state marine police forces will collaborate to operate and maintain these integrated surveillance assets.
• The move underscores India's commitment to securing its maritime borders and protecting its strategic economic interests in the Arabian Sea.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: New surveillance systems deployed along Western Coastline.
Integrates radar, electro-optic sensors, and UAVs.
Aims to counter infiltration, smuggling, and maritime terrorism.
Collaboration between Navy, Coast Guard, and marine police.
Western Ghats is a biodiversity hotspot.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted threats to India's coastal security and the strategies employed to address them.
Analyze the role of technology, such as integrated surveillance systems, in enhancing maritime domain awareness and coastal defense.
Examine the challenges in inter-agency coordination among various forces involved in coastal security and suggest measures for better synergy.
Evaluate the economic and strategic implications of robust coastal security for India's trade, energy security, and regional influence.
articles.imageDescription: A patrol boat of the Indian Coast Guard on the ocean, with a radar tower in the background, symbolizing coastal surveillance.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, making coastal security a complex and critical aspect of national defense.

articles.headline: Government Launches Nationwide Campaign for Digital Literacy in Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today launched a comprehensive nationwide campaign aimed at boosting digital literacy across India's rural populace.
• The initiative focuses on empowering citizens with essential digital skills, including safe internet usage, online banking, and accessing government e-services.
• The campaign employs a multi-pronged approach, utilizing local common service centers (CSCs), community educators, and digital learning modules in regional languages.
• It seeks to bridge the urban-rural digital divide and ensure that the benefits of the Digital India program reach every corner of the country.
• This move is expected to enhance financial inclusion, promote entrepreneurship, and improve access to education and healthcare services in remote areas.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Human Resources
Development and Management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
E-governance
prelimsPoints: Nationwide campaign for digital literacy launched by MeitY.
Aims to empower rural citizens with digital skills.
Utilizes CSCs and community educators.
Seeks to bridge the urban-rural digital divide.
Promotes financial inclusion and e-governance access.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of digital literacy in achieving inclusive growth and empowering rural communities in India.
Analyze the challenges in delivering digital education to remote areas and the strategies employed by the government to overcome them.
Evaluate the potential impact of enhanced digital literacy on financial inclusion, access to government services, and local entrepreneurship.
Examine how the 'Digital India' program contributes to bridging the digital divide and fostering a digitally empowered society.
articles.imageDescription: A group of rural individuals learning to use digital devices, symbolizing digital empowerment.
articles.trivia: The Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA) is one of the world's largest digital literacy programs, aiming to make 6 crore rural households digitally literate.

articles.headline: National Conference Discusses Urban Heat Island Effect Mitigation Strategies
articles.explainer:
• A national conference on 'Urban Resilience to Climate Change' concluded today, with key discussions centered on mitigating the escalating Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in Indian cities.
• Experts highlighted the adverse impacts of UHIs, including increased energy consumption, exacerbated heat-related illnesses, and compromised air quality.
• Strategies proposed included promoting green infrastructure, using cool pavements and reflective surfaces, and improving urban planning with a focus on sustainable building materials.
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs emphasized the need for integrated urban planning approaches that incorporate climate resilience measures from the design stage.
• The conference called for greater public awareness and citizen participation in adopting sustainable urban living practices to combat rising urban temperatures.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Climate Change
prelimsPoints: UHI effect refers to warmer temperatures in urban areas compared to rural surroundings.
Caused by heat absorption by concrete, asphalt, and lack of vegetation.
Mitigation strategies include green infrastructure, cool surfaces.
Impacts include increased energy use and health risks.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs focuses on urban planning.
mainsPoints: Explain the phenomenon of Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect and its major causes in rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Discuss the environmental, health, and economic impacts of UHIs on urban populations and infrastructure.
Analyze various mitigation strategies, including green infrastructure, cool pavements, and policy interventions, to reduce the UHI effect.
Evaluate the role of urban planning and governance in building climate-resilient cities in India, considering the challenges of rapid urbanization.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a city showing a mix of concrete structures and green spaces, highlighting the contrast in urban temperatures.
articles.trivia: The Urban Heat Island effect can raise city temperatures by several degrees Celsius, significantly impacting energy consumption for cooling and increasing heat-related mortality during heatwaves.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Urgent UN Security Council Reforms at General Assembly Debate
articles.explainer:
• India today strongly reiterated its call for urgent and comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) during a high-level debate at the UN General Assembly.
• India's Permanent Representative highlighted the outdated structure of the UNSC, arguing that it no longer reflects the contemporary geopolitical realities and the increased representation of developing nations.
• The demand included an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent categories of membership, with India seeking a permanent seat to reflect its growing global stature and contributions.
• Other member states from the G4 group (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) also echoed similar sentiments, emphasizing the need for a more equitable and effective multilateral system.
• The debate underscored the ongoing challenges in achieving consensus on UNSC reforms, despite widespread recognition of its necessity.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: India advocates for UNSC reforms.
UNSC has 5 permanent (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
P5 members have veto power.
India is part of the G4 group seeking permanent seats.
UNGA is the main deliberative body of the UN.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the reform of the UN Security Council, with a specific focus on India's claim for a permanent seat.
Analyze how the current structure of the UNSC impacts its effectiveness in addressing contemporary global challenges.
Examine the role of multilateral forums like the G4 in pushing for UNSC reforms and the obstacles they face.
Evaluate India's growing role in global affairs and its contributions that justify a permanent seat in the UN Security Council.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations headquarters building in New York, symbolizing international diplomacy and cooperation.
articles.trivia: The last major reform of the UN Security Council occurred in 1965 when the number of non-permanent members was increased from six to ten.

articles.headline: Export Promotion Council Highlights Challenges and Opportunities for Indian MSMEs in Global Markets
articles.explainer:
• The Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO) today released a report highlighting both significant opportunities and persistent challenges faced by Indian Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) in expanding their footprint in global markets.
• The report identified e-commerce platforms, free trade agreements (FTAs), and diversification into new product categories as key growth avenues for MSMEs.
• Major challenges include access to affordable credit, compliance with international standards, logistical hurdles, and limited awareness of export procedures.
• The government was urged to provide more targeted support, including capacity building, technology upgrades, and easier access to export finance.
• Enhancing MSME exports is crucial for India's overall trade balance, job creation, and integration into global value chains.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Investment models
prelimsPoints: FIEO reports on MSME export challenges and opportunities.
E-commerce and FTAs are growth avenues for MSMEs.
Challenges include credit access, compliance, logistics.
MSMEs are vital for India's trade balance and job creation.
Government support is crucial for MSME export growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of MSMEs in India's economy and their potential contribution to achieving the country's export targets.
Analyze the major barriers hindering Indian MSMEs from effectively competing in global markets and expanding their export base.
Evaluate the role of government policies, trade agreements, and digital platforms in facilitating MSME exports.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to enhance the competitiveness of Indian MSMEs in global value chains and address their structural challenges.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing global trade routes and small businesses, representing MSME participation in international commerce.
articles.trivia: MSMEs contribute approximately 30% to India's GDP and about 45% to its manufacturing output, employing a significant portion of the workforce.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Announce Breakthrough in Quantum Computing Research
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists from a leading research institution today announced a significant breakthrough in quantum computing, achieving a new milestone in qubit stability and error correction.
• The research focused on developing more robust quantum bits (qubits) and enhancing their coherence time, which is critical for building practical quantum computers.
• This advancement has implications for various fields, including drug discovery, materials science, financial modeling, and cryptography, promising to solve complex problems currently intractable for classical computers.
• The Ministry of Science & Technology lauded the achievement, emphasizing India's growing capabilities in cutting-edge technologies under the National Quantum Mission.
• Further research aims to scale up the quantum systems and integrate them with existing high-performance computing infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
IT, Computers, Robotics, Nanotechnology, Biotechnology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Indian scientists made a breakthrough in quantum computing.
Focus on qubit stability and error correction.
Quantum computing can solve complex problems faster.
National Quantum Mission supports research in this area.
Qubits are the basic units of quantum information.
mainsPoints: Explain the fundamental principles of quantum computing and how it differs from classical computing.
Discuss the potential applications of quantum technology across various sectors, including healthcare, finance, and defense.
Analyze the challenges in developing practical quantum computers and India's efforts under the National Quantum Mission to overcome them.
Evaluate the ethical and security implications of advancements in quantum computing, particularly in cryptography and data privacy.
articles.imageDescription: An abstract representation of quantum entanglement, symbolizing quantum computing and advanced technology.
articles.trivia: Unlike classical computers that use bits (0 or 1), quantum computers use qubits which can exist in multiple states simultaneously (superposition), allowing for exponentially more complex calculations.

articles.headline: State Government Introduces New Bill to Streamline Public Service Delivery
articles.explainer:
• A major state government today introduced a new Public Service Delivery Bill in its legislative assembly, aimed at enhancing efficiency, transparency, and accountability in the provision of government services.
• The proposed legislation mandates time-bound delivery of various public services, including permits, certificates, and welfare benefits, and introduces a grievance redressal mechanism for citizens.
• It seeks to empower citizens by providing them with a legal right to public services and imposing penalties on officials for unjustified delays or denial of services.
• The bill also proposes the adoption of e-governance platforms and single-window systems to simplify procedures and reduce bureaucratic hurdles.
• This initiative is expected to significantly improve citizen-centric governance and reduce corruption at the local level.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Transparency and Accountability and institutional and other measures
Citizens Charters, Transparency & Accountability and institutional and other measures
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: New bill introduced to streamline public service delivery.
Mandates time-bound delivery of government services.
Includes grievance redressal mechanism.
Proposes e-governance and single-window systems.
Aims to improve citizen-centric governance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Right to Public Services Act in promoting good governance and empowering citizens.
Analyze the challenges in effective public service delivery in India and how legislative measures like this bill seek to address them.
Evaluate the role of e-governance and technology in improving transparency, accountability, and efficiency in government services.
Suggest reforms to further strengthen citizen-centric governance and ensure timely and corruption-free delivery of public services.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a hand holding a document, with various government service icons in the background, representing efficient public service.
articles.trivia: Several Indian states have already enacted their own Right to Public Services Acts, with Madhya Pradesh being the first in 2010.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers Ancient Trade Route Artifacts in Southern India
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) today announced the discovery of a significant cache of artifacts along what is believed to be an ancient trade route in a remote region of Southern India.
• The excavations unearthed pottery, coins, and remnants of ancient structures, providing crucial insights into the historical trade networks that connected the Indian subcontinent with other civilizations.
• Preliminary analysis suggests the artifacts date back to the early common era, indicating active commerce and cultural exchange with Roman and Southeast Asian regions.
• The discovery is expected to shed new light on the economic history and maritime trade routes of ancient India, potentially altering existing historical narratives.
• Further research and conservation efforts are underway to preserve the site and meticulously study the unearthed treasures.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture
History of India
Ancient India
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered artifacts on an ancient trade route in Southern India.
Artifacts include pottery, coins, ancient structures.
Date back to the early common era.
Indicate trade with Roman and Southeast Asian regions.
ASI is responsible for archaeological research and heritage preservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the economic and cultural history of ancient India.
Analyze the nature of ancient trade routes connecting India with the Roman Empire and Southeast Asia, based on archaeological evidence.
Evaluate how such findings contribute to our understanding of India's role in global commerce and cultural exchange during ancient times.
Examine the challenges faced by ASI in preserving and studying archaeological sites in India and suggest measures for their effective management.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists at an excavation site, carefully uncovering ancient artifacts from the soil.
articles.trivia: The ancient port city of Arikamedu near Pondicherry was a major Indo-Roman trading station, with excavations revealing Roman pottery, glass, and gems.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Hold Productive Dialogue on Fisheries Dispute and Maritime Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka today concluded a constructive round of talks aimed at resolving the long-standing fisheries dispute and enhancing overall maritime cooperation.
• Discussions focused on practical solutions for preventing illegal fishing, protecting the livelihoods of fishermen from both nations, and exploring joint surveillance mechanisms.
• Both sides reiterated the importance of adhering to existing agreements and finding humanitarian solutions for fishermen apprehended across maritime boundaries.
• The dialogue also touched upon broader regional security concerns and collaboration in marine environmental protection initiatives.
• This engagement reflects ongoing efforts to strengthen bilateral ties and foster a peaceful resolution to shared maritime challenges in the Palk Strait region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Sri Lanka held talks on fisheries dispute.
Dispute involves fishermen in the Palk Strait.
Focus on illegal fishing and livelihood protection.
Katchatheevu Island is a point of contention.
Aims for humanitarian solutions for apprehended fishermen.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and complexities of the India-Sri Lanka fisheries dispute in the Palk Strait.
Discuss the socio-economic impact of the dispute on the fishing communities of both countries and the environmental concerns associated with unsustainable fishing practices.
Evaluate the various diplomatic and legal approaches adopted by India and Sri Lanka to resolve the issue.
Suggest sustainable and equitable solutions that can address the livelihood concerns of fishermen while preserving marine resources and strengthening bilateral relations.
articles.imageDescription: Fishing boats in the Palk Strait, with the flags of India and Sri Lanka in the background, symbolizing the shared maritime boundary.
articles.trivia: The Katchatheevu Island, ceded by India to Sri Lanka in 1974, remains a contentious point in the fisheries dispute, as Indian fishermen traditionally fished in its surrounding waters.

articles.headline: India on Track to Exceed Renewable Energy Targets by 2030, Says Ministry of Power
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power today announced that India is well on its way to not only meet but potentially exceed its ambitious renewable energy targets set for 2030.
• The accelerated pace of solar and wind power installations, coupled with supportive government policies and private sector investment, has been credited for this progress.
• India aims to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030, a key commitment made at international climate forums.
• The expansion of renewable energy is crucial for India's energy security, reducing carbon emissions, and combating climate change.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, storage solutions, and land acquisition, but concerted efforts are being made to address these bottlenecks.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Climate Change
prelimsPoints: India aims for 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Progress driven by solar and wind power installations.
Ministry of Power oversees energy sector.
Challenges include grid integration and storage.
Renewable energy is vital for climate change goals.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's ambitious renewable energy targets and the progress made towards achieving them, highlighting key policy drivers.
Analyze the socio-economic and environmental benefits of transitioning to renewable energy for India.
Examine the challenges associated with large-scale renewable energy integration into the national grid and the development of adequate storage solutions.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and private investment in accelerating India's renewable energy transition.
articles.imageDescription: A vast solar power farm stretching to the horizon, symbolizing renewable energy generation.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of renewable energy in the world, with significant investments in solar and wind power projects.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) Expands, Focus on Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) today announced the further expansion of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), with a renewed focus on integrating healthcare facilities in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The mission aims to create a national digital health ecosystem, enabling seamless online access to health records, teleconsultations, and other digital health services.
• Key components include the generation of Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) for citizens and the digitization of health records across public and private healthcare providers.
• This expansion seeks to improve accessibility, efficiency, and equity in healthcare delivery, particularly for underserved populations.
• Challenges related to digital infrastructure, data privacy, and public awareness are being addressed through targeted campaigns and technological upgrades.
gsSubTopics: Development and Management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: ABDM is expanding, focusing on Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
Aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
Key components include ABHA IDs and digital health records.
NHA is the implementing agency for ABDM.
Improves healthcare accessibility and efficiency.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key features of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) and its potential to revolutionize healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze the benefits of a national digital health ecosystem for citizens, healthcare providers, and policymakers.
Examine the challenges associated with the implementation of ABDM, including data privacy concerns, digital literacy, and infrastructure gaps.
Suggest measures to ensure the equitable and secure expansion of ABDM, particularly in rural and underserved areas.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital health icons, symbolizing the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) was launched nationwide in September 2021, building upon the pilot project in six Union Territories.

articles.headline: Editorial: Paving the Way for Green Manufacturing in India's Economic Future
articles.explainer:
• Today's editorial analyzes the government's new 'India Innovation & Manufacturing Boost' (IIMB) policy, focusing on its emphasis on green manufacturing practices.
• It argues that integrating sustainability into industrial policy is not just an environmental imperative but also an economic opportunity for India to gain a competitive edge in global markets.
• The editorial highlights the potential for job creation in green sectors and the importance of technological innovation in achieving cleaner production processes.
• It calls for robust regulatory frameworks, financial incentives, and capacity building to ensure effective adoption of green technologies by industries, especially MSMEs.
• The piece concludes by stressing that a sustainable manufacturing future requires a collaborative effort from government, industry, and research institutions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Industrial Policy
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of green manufacturing and its significance for India's economic and environmental goals.
Analyze how government policies, such as the IIMB, can incentivize industries to adopt sustainable production practices.
Evaluate the economic benefits and challenges of transitioning to a green manufacturing economy, including job creation and technological requirements.
Examine the role of various stakeholders, including government, industry, and consumers, in fostering a sustainable manufacturing ecosystem in India.
articles.imageDescription: A factory emitting clean air, with green energy symbols, representing sustainable industrial practices.
articles.trivia: Green manufacturing aims to minimize negative environmental impacts while conserving energy and natural resources, aligning with the principles of sustainable development.

highlights: Government launches 'India Innovation & Manufacturing Boost' (IIMB) policy to propel domestic production and R&D.
ISRO successfully conducts a key test for its next-gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator.
Supreme Court issues comprehensive guidelines to safeguard prisoner rights and ensure access to legal aid.
India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concludes, focusing on Indo-Pacific security and enhanced economic cooperation.
New Tiger Census data released, showing population growth but highlighting critical conservation challenges.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'India Innovation & Manufacturing Boost' (IIMB) policy recently in news, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to significantly enhance domestic manufacturing capabilities and foster a robust ecosystem for research and development.
2. It introduces production-linked incentives (PLI) for new sectors and tax breaks for R&D investments.
3. The policy primarily focuses on heavy industries and excludes Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the IIMB policy aims to boost domestic manufacturing and R&D. Statement 2 is correct as it includes PLI schemes and tax breaks for R&D investments. Statement 3 is incorrect because the policy explicitly mentions easier access to credit for MSMEs in manufacturing, indicating their inclusion, not exclusion.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the primary objective of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) program?
options: (a) To launch heavy communication satellites into geostationary orbit.
(b) To develop a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle to drastically reduce the cost of space access.
(c) To conduct deep space manned missions to other planets.
(d) To serve as a platform for atmospheric research and weather forecasting.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To develop a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle to drastically reduce the cost of space access.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The RLV-TD program's core objective is to develop a fully reusable launch vehicle, which is crucial for significantly reducing the cost of accessing space, thereby making space missions more economical and sustainable. Options (a), (c), and (d) describe other potential applications or unrelated objectives, not the primary goal of RLV-TD.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India:
1. Article 44 of the Indian Constitution mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC for its citizens.
2. Goa is currently the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
3. The UCC aims to replace personal laws based on religious scriptures and customs with a common set of laws for all citizens.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Article 44 of the Constitution is a Directive Principle of State Policy calling for a UCC. Statement 2 is correct: Goa is indeed the only state in India with a UCC. Statement 3 is also correct: The fundamental aim of a UCC is to establish common personal laws for marriage, divorce, inheritance, adoption, etc., for all citizens, irrespective of their religion.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following groups of nations is collectively known as the 'G4' and advocates for reforms in the UN Security Council, including seeking permanent membership?
options: (d) Canada, Australia, New Zealand, South Korea
prelimsMocks.explanation: The G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, and Japan) are four countries that support each other's bids for permanent seats on the United Nations Security Council. They are all among the largest economies in the world and frequently contribute troops to UN peacekeeping missions.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Urban Heat Island' (UHI) effect, often discussed in environmental studies, primarily refers to:
options: (a) The phenomenon of increased biodiversity in urban green spaces compared to rural areas.
(b) The tendency of urban areas to be significantly warmer than their surrounding rural areas.
(c) The concentration of industrial pollution in specific urban zones leading to higher temperatures.
(d) The impact of global warming causing sea-level rise in coastal cities.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) The tendency of urban areas to be significantly warmer than their surrounding rural areas.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect is a phenomenon where urban and metropolitan areas are significantly warmer than their surrounding rural areas due to human activities. This temperature difference is caused by factors like dark surfaces (asphalt, concrete) absorbing solar radiation, lack of vegetation, and heat generated by human activities and infrastructure.

mainsMocks.question: The 'India Innovation & Manufacturing Boost' (IIMB) policy aims to position India as a global manufacturing hub. Discuss the potential of this policy to drive economic growth and job creation, while also analyzing the key challenges in its implementation. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Industrial Policy.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce IIMB policy and its 'Make in India' alignment. Discuss potential benefits: economic growth, job creation, reduced import dependency, R&D boost. Analyze challenges: infrastructure gaps, skill development, access to finance for MSMEs, global competition, technological adoption. Conclude with need for robust implementation.

mainsMocks.question: Despite a growing tiger population, the latest census data highlights emerging challenges in tiger conservation. Critically examine these challenges and suggest comprehensive strategies for ensuring the long-term survival of tigers in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Biodiversity.
mainsMocks.approach: Acknowledge success of Project Tiger and recent growth. Identify challenges: habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, poaching (though reduced), climate change impacts, lack of corridor connectivity, resource pressure. Suggest strategies: community participation, eco-development, robust anti-poaching, scientific management, climate-resilient habitats, inter-state/international cooperation, policy enforcement.

mainsMocks.question: India's consistent advocacy for reforms in the UN Security Council underscores its aspiration for a greater global role. Discuss the arguments for expanding the UNSC's permanent membership, particularly India's claim, and analyze the major obstacles hindering such reforms. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by stating India's aspiration and the outdated UNSC structure. Present arguments for expansion: reflects current geopolitical realities, increased representation for developing nations, enhanced legitimacy and effectiveness. Detail India's claim: large population, democratic values, economic power, contributions to UN peacekeeping, responsible nuclear power. Analyze obstacles: P5's reluctance to dilute power (especially veto), lack of consensus among non-permanent members, regional rivalries, complex amendment process for UN Charter. Conclude on the necessity of reforms for a more multilateral world order.

quickBytes.text: The 'India Innovation & Manufacturing Boost' (IIMB) policy aims to integrate advanced technologies like AI and IoT.
Article 44 of the Indian Constitution is a Directive Principle of State Policy related to the Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) aims to significantly reduce space launch costs.
The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is the statutory body for tiger conservation in India.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the RBI is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate (repo rate).
Article 39A of the Indian Constitution mandates free legal aid for equitable justice.
The Palk Strait separates India and Sri Lanka, often a point of contention over fishing rights.
India aims to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by the year 2030.
The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) creates Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) for citizens.
Green manufacturing focuses on minimizing environmental impacts and conserving resources in industrial processes.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers Ancient Trade Route Artifacts in Southern India
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) today announced the discovery of a significant cache of artifacts along what is believed to be an ancient trade route in a remote region of Southern India.
• The excavations unearthed pottery, coins, and remnants of ancient structures, providing crucial insights into the historical trade networks that connected the Indian subcontinent with other civilizations.
• Preliminary analysis suggests the artifacts date back to the early common era, indicating active commerce and cultural exchange with Roman and Southeast Asian regions.
• The discovery is expected to shed new light on the economic history and maritime trade routes of ancient India, potentially altering existing historical narratives.
• Further research and conservation efforts are underway to preserve the site and meticulously study the unearthed treasures.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture
History of India
Ancient India
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered artifacts on an ancient trade route in Southern India.
Artifacts include pottery, coins, ancient structures.
Date back to the early common era.
Indicate trade with Roman and Southeast Asian regions.
ASI is responsible for archaeological research and heritage preservation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the economic and cultural history of ancient India.
Analyze the nature of ancient trade routes connecting India with the Roman Empire and Southeast Asia, based on archaeological evidence.
Evaluate how such findings contribute to our understanding of India's role in global commerce and cultural exchange during ancient times.
Examine the challenges faced by ASI in preserving and studying archaeological sites in India and suggest measures for their effective management.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists at an excavation site, carefully uncovering ancient artifacts from the soil.
GS1.trivia: The ancient port city of Arikamedu near Pondicherry was a major Indo-Roman trading station, with excavations revealing Roman pottery, glass, and gems.

GS2.headline: Parliament Winter Session: Heated Debates on Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Progress
GS2.explainer:
• The ongoing Winter Session of Parliament witnessed intense discussions and debates today regarding the potential enactment and implications of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC).
• Members from various political parties presented their arguments, with proponents emphasizing national unity and gender justice, while opponents raised concerns about religious freedom and cultural diversity.
• The Law Commission's recent consultations and recommendations on UCC were frequently cited during the parliamentary proceedings.
• The government reiterated its commitment to Article 44 of the Constitution, which mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC for its citizens.
• The debate highlighted the complex socio-political landscape surrounding personal laws in India and the challenges in achieving consensus.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: UCC is mentioned in Article 44 of the Indian Constitution.
It is a Directive Principle of State Policy (DPSP).
Aims for common personal laws for all citizens, regardless of religion.
Law Commission of India advises the government on legal reforms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional validity and socio-political implications of implementing a Uniform Civil Code in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against UCC, considering aspects of gender justice, national integration, and protection of minority rights.
Examine the role of the judiciary and the legislature in progressing towards a UCC, citing past judgments and debates.
Suggest a roadmap for achieving a consensual and inclusive Uniform Civil Code in a diverse country like India.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Parliament House of India, symbolizing legislative debates and policy-making.
GS2.trivia: Goa is currently the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from its Portuguese colonial past.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizing Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded today in New Delhi, reaffirming the commitment of both nations to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and counter-terrorism efforts, alongside exploring new avenues for collaboration in critical technologies and supply chain resilience.
• Both sides stressed the importance of upholding international law, particularly the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), in addressing regional challenges.
• Economic partnerships, including infrastructure development projects in India and trade facilitation, were also key agenda items.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan in navigating the complex geopolitical landscape of Asia.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India and Japan hold annual Strategic Dialogues.
Focus on Indo-Pacific security and economic ties.
Both nations are members of the QUAD grouping.
Cooperation areas include defense, maritime security, technology, and infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan strategic partnership for regional stability and economic development in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how India's 'Act East Policy' and Japan's 'Free and Open Indo-Pacific' vision converge to address geopolitical challenges.
Evaluate the progress and potential of defense and security cooperation between India and Japan, particularly in the maritime domain.
Examine the role of India-Japan collaboration in building resilient supply chains and promoting critical technologies.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed together, symbolizing diplomatic relations and cooperation.
GS2.trivia: Japan is one of the largest investors in India, particularly in infrastructure projects like the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail corridor.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Issues Comprehensive Guidelines on Prisoner Rights and Access to Legal Aid
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a landmark judgment, issuing a comprehensive set of guidelines aimed at safeguarding the rights of prisoners and ensuring their effective access to legal aid.
• The directives include mandates for timely legal representation from the moment of arrest, regular medical check-ups, and provisions for humane treatment within correctional facilities.
• The Court emphasized the importance of prison reforms, urging both central and state governments to allocate adequate resources for improving prison infrastructure and staff training.
• The judgment also highlighted the need for greater transparency in prison administration and the establishment of independent oversight mechanisms.
• This ruling is expected to have a far-reaching impact on India's criminal justice system, particularly in addressing issues of overcrowding and human rights violations in prisons.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
keyTerms: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court issued guidelines on prisoner rights.
Mandates timely legal representation and humane treatment.
Stresses on prison reforms and resource allocation.
Emphasizes transparency and independent oversight in prisons.
Article 21 guarantees right to life and personal liberty.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional basis and significance of the Supreme Court's guidelines on prisoner rights and legal aid.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Indian prison system, such as overcrowding, understaffing, and human rights violations, and how these guidelines address them.
Evaluate the role of legal aid services in ensuring access to justice for vulnerable sections, particularly undertrial prisoners.
Suggest comprehensive reforms for the Indian criminal justice system, encompassing policing, judiciary, and correctional administration, in light of the Supreme Court's directives.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building, representing judicial pronouncements and justice.
GS2.trivia: Article 39A of the Indian Constitution directs the State to provide free legal aid to ensure that opportunities for securing justice are not denied to any citizen by reason of economic or other disabilities.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Supreme Court judgments and their impact

GS2.headline: Government Launches Nationwide Campaign for Digital Literacy in Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today launched a comprehensive nationwide campaign aimed at boosting digital literacy across India's rural populace.
• The initiative focuses on empowering citizens with essential digital skills, including safe internet usage, online banking, and accessing government e-services.
• The campaign employs a multi-pronged approach, utilizing local common service centers (CSCs), community educators, and digital learning modules in regional languages.
• It seeks to bridge the urban-rural digital divide and ensure that the benefits of the Digital India program reach every corner of the country.
• This move is expected to enhance financial inclusion, promote entrepreneurship, and improve access to education and healthcare services in remote areas.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Human Resources
Development and Management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
E-governance
prelimsPoints: Nationwide campaign for digital literacy launched by MeitY.
Aims to empower rural citizens with digital skills.
Utilizes CSCs and community educators.
Seeks to bridge the urban-rural digital divide.
Promotes financial inclusion and e-governance access.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of digital literacy in achieving inclusive growth and empowering rural communities in India.
Analyze the challenges in delivering digital education to remote areas and the strategies employed by the government to overcome them.
Evaluate the potential impact of enhanced digital literacy on financial inclusion, access to government services, and local entrepreneurship.
Examine how the 'Digital India' program contributes to bridging the digital divide and fostering a digitally empowered society.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of rural individuals learning to use digital devices, symbolizing digital empowerment.
GS2.trivia: The Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA) is one of the world's largest digital literacy programs, aiming to make 6 crore rural households digitally literate.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Urgent UN Security Council Reforms at General Assembly Debate
GS2.explainer:
• India today strongly reiterated its call for urgent and comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) during a high-level debate at the UN General Assembly.
• India's Permanent Representative highlighted the outdated structure of the UNSC, arguing that it no longer reflects the contemporary geopolitical realities and the increased representation of developing nations.
• The demand included an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent categories of membership, with India seeking a permanent seat to reflect its growing global stature and contributions.
• Other member states from the G4 group (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) also echoed similar sentiments, emphasizing the need for a more equitable and effective multilateral system.
• The debate underscored the ongoing challenges in achieving consensus on UNSC reforms, despite widespread recognition of its necessity.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: India advocates for UNSC reforms.
UNSC has 5 permanent (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
P5 members have veto power.
India is part of the G4 group seeking permanent seats.
UNGA is the main deliberative body of the UN.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the reform of the UN Security Council, with a specific focus on India's claim for a permanent seat.
Analyze how the current structure of the UNSC impacts its effectiveness in addressing contemporary global challenges.
Examine the role of multilateral forums like the G4 in pushing for UNSC reforms and the obstacles they face.
Evaluate India's growing role in global affairs and its contributions that justify a permanent seat in the UN Security Council.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations headquarters building in New York, symbolizing international diplomacy and cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The last major reform of the UN Security Council occurred in 1965 when the number of non-permanent members was increased from six to ten.

GS2.headline: State Government Introduces New Bill to Streamline Public Service Delivery
GS2.explainer:
• A major state government today introduced a new Public Service Delivery Bill in its legislative assembly, aimed at enhancing efficiency, transparency, and accountability in the provision of government services.
• The proposed legislation mandates time-bound delivery of various public services, including permits, certificates, and welfare benefits, and introduces a grievance redressal mechanism for citizens.
• It seeks to empower citizens by providing them with a legal right to public services and imposing penalties on officials for unjustified delays or denial of services.
• The bill also proposes the adoption of e-governance platforms and single-window systems to simplify procedures and reduce bureaucratic hurdles.
• This initiative is expected to significantly improve citizen-centric governance and reduce corruption at the local level.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Transparency and Accountability and institutional and other measures
Citizens Charters, Transparency & Accountability and institutional and other measures
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: New bill introduced to streamline public service delivery.
Mandates time-bound delivery of government services.
Includes grievance redressal mechanism.
Proposes e-governance and single-window systems.
Aims to improve citizen-centric governance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Right to Public Services Act in promoting good governance and empowering citizens.
Analyze the challenges in effective public service delivery in India and how legislative measures like this bill seek to address them.
Evaluate the role of e-governance and technology in improving transparency, accountability, and efficiency in government services.
Suggest reforms to further strengthen citizen-centric governance and ensure timely and corruption-free delivery of public services.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized image of a hand holding a document, with various government service icons in the background, representing efficient public service.
GS2.trivia: Several Indian states have already enacted their own Right to Public Services Acts, with Madhya Pradesh being the first in 2010.

GS2.headline: India and Sri Lanka Hold Productive Dialogue on Fisheries Dispute and Maritime Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka today concluded a constructive round of talks aimed at resolving the long-standing fisheries dispute and enhancing overall maritime cooperation.
• Discussions focused on practical solutions for preventing illegal fishing, protecting the livelihoods of fishermen from both nations, and exploring joint surveillance mechanisms.
• Both sides reiterated the importance of adhering to existing agreements and finding humanitarian solutions for fishermen apprehended across maritime boundaries.
• The dialogue also touched upon broader regional security concerns and collaboration in marine environmental protection initiatives.
• This engagement reflects ongoing efforts to strengthen bilateral ties and foster a peaceful resolution to shared maritime challenges in the Palk Strait region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Sri Lanka held talks on fisheries dispute.
Dispute involves fishermen in the Palk Strait.
Focus on illegal fishing and livelihood protection.
Katchatheevu Island is a point of contention.
Aims for humanitarian solutions for apprehended fishermen.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and complexities of the India-Sri Lanka fisheries dispute in the Palk Strait.
Discuss the socio-economic impact of the dispute on the fishing communities of both countries and the environmental concerns associated with unsustainable fishing practices.
Evaluate the various diplomatic and legal approaches adopted by India and Sri Lanka to resolve the issue.
Suggest sustainable and equitable solutions that can address the livelihood concerns of fishermen while preserving marine resources and strengthening bilateral relations.
GS2.imageDescription: Fishing boats in the Palk Strait, with the flags of India and Sri Lanka in the background, symbolizing the shared maritime boundary.
GS2.trivia: The Katchatheevu Island, ceded by India to Sri Lanka in 1974, remains a contentious point in the fisheries dispute, as Indian fishermen traditionally fished in its surrounding waters.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) Expands, Focus on Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) today announced the further expansion of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), with a renewed focus on integrating healthcare facilities in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The mission aims to create a national digital health ecosystem, enabling seamless online access to health records, teleconsultations, and other digital health services.
• Key components include the generation of Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) for citizens and the digitization of health records across public and private healthcare providers.
• This expansion seeks to improve accessibility, efficiency, and equity in healthcare delivery, particularly for underserved populations.
• Challenges related to digital infrastructure, data privacy, and public awareness are being addressed through targeted campaigns and technological upgrades.
gsSubTopics: Development and Management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: ABDM is expanding, focusing on Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
Aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
Key components include ABHA IDs and digital health records.
NHA is the implementing agency for ABDM.
Improves healthcare accessibility and efficiency.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key features of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) and its potential to revolutionize healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze the benefits of a national digital health ecosystem for citizens, healthcare providers, and policymakers.
Examine the challenges associated with the implementation of ABDM, including data privacy concerns, digital literacy, and infrastructure gaps.
Suggest measures to ensure the equitable and secure expansion of ABDM, particularly in rural and underserved areas.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital health icons, symbolizing the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) was launched nationwide in September 2021, building upon the pilot project in six Union Territories.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'India Innovation & Manufacturing Boost' (IIMB) Policy to Catalyse Domestic Production and R&D
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the ambitious 'India Innovation & Manufacturing Boost' (IIMB) policy, aimed at significantly enhancing domestic manufacturing capabilities and fostering a robust ecosystem for research and development.
• The policy introduces a package of incentives, including production-linked incentives (PLI) for new sectors, tax breaks for R&D investments, and easier access to credit for MSMEs in manufacturing.
• A key focus is on integrating advanced technologies like AI, IoT, and automation into traditional manufacturing processes to improve efficiency and competitiveness.
• The IIMB policy seeks to create millions of new jobs, reduce import dependency, and position India as a global manufacturing hub, aligning with the 'Make in India' vision.
• Special provisions are included to promote green manufacturing practices and circular economy principles within the industrial sector.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Industrial Policy
Make in India
Investment models
prelimsPoints: IIMB policy is launched by the Union Government.
Aims to boost domestic manufacturing and R&D.
Includes PLI schemes and tax breaks for R&D.
Focuses on integrating AI, IoT, automation.
Promotes green manufacturing and circular economy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of the IIMB policy to transform India's manufacturing sector and its contribution to GDP.
Analyze the challenges in implementing such a large-scale policy and suggest measures for effective execution.
Evaluate the role of technology integration (AI, IoT) in enhancing India's industrial competitiveness under the new policy.
Examine how the IIMB policy aligns with broader government initiatives like 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of factories with gears and innovation symbols, representing industrial growth and technological advancement.
GS3.trivia: India aims to increase the manufacturing sector's share in GDP to 25% by 2025 under the National Manufacturing Policy, a goal further supported by initiatives like IIMB.

GS3.headline: New Tiger Census Data Released: Highlights Challenges in Conservation Efforts Despite Population Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The latest All India Tiger Estimation (AITE) report, commonly known as the Tiger Census, was released today, indicating a continued increase in India's tiger population.
• While the overall numbers show a positive trend, the report highlighted significant regional disparities and emerging challenges such as habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching.
• The census employed advanced methodologies, including camera trapping and DNA analysis, to provide a more accurate count and ecological insights.
• Conservationists called for enhanced protection measures, community involvement, and corridor connectivity to ensure the long-term survival of tigers outside protected areas.
• The report serves as a crucial benchmark for evaluating the effectiveness of 'Project Tiger' and informs future conservation strategies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Wildlife Protection Act
prelimsPoints: Tiger Census is conducted every four years in India.
NTCA is responsible for Project Tiger and tiger conservation.
India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
Methods include camera trapping, DNA analysis, and pugmark estimation.
Challenges include habitat loss, poaching, and human-wildlife conflict.
mainsPoints: Analyze the successes and failures of Project Tiger based on the latest census data, highlighting key regions of concern.
Discuss the socio-economic dimensions of human-wildlife conflict and suggest sustainable solutions for co-existence.
Evaluate the role of protected areas and wildlife corridors in maintaining genetic diversity and ensuring tiger population viability.
Examine how climate change and developmental projects pose threats to tiger habitats and conservation efforts in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger in its natural habitat, symbolizing wildlife conservation.
GS3.trivia: India's tiger population has more than doubled since the launch of Project Tiger in 1973, making it one of the most successful conservation programs globally.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
• The test involved a precise landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, demonstrating key technologies for autonomous navigation, guidance, and control.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, aimed at drastically reducing the cost of space access.
• The RLV-TD program is part of ISRO's long-term vision to enhance India's capabilities in space transportation and make space missions more economical and sustainable.
• Future tests will focus on orbital re-entry and hypersonic flight capabilities, paving the way for advanced space exploration and satellite deployment.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested its RLV-TD.
RLV aims to reduce space launch costs.
Test involved autonomous landing simulation.
RLV-TD is a step towards a fully reusable orbital vehicle.
ISRO is India's national space agency.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze how RLV technology can contribute to making space missions more economical and sustainable.
